Expected Result: Supplier name should be always "Farmart services private limited"

Test Case Description: To verify the Billing From screen

Test Case Description: To verify the Supplier Field in Biiling from screen
Expected Result: The system should display the supplier as a Farmart for all the orders and it should not be editable

Expected Result: The system should display all the addresses of  Farmart in the dropdown  and it should be editable.

Test Case Description: To verify the Pincode field with invalid data
Expected Result: System should not allow to save the changes and give proper error message

Test Case Description: Check if the Supplier field displays "Farmart Service Private Limited" by default.
Expected Result: The Supplier field is pre-filled with "Farmart Service Private Limited".

Test Case Description: Open the Address dropdown and check if all available addresses for Farmart are displayed.
Expected Result: The dropdown lists all valid Farmart addresses.

Test Case Description: Select an address from the dropdown and verify if the Pincode, District, and State fields autofill correctly.
Expected Result: Pincode, District, and State fields are autofilled based on the selected address.

Test Case Description: Select a valid address, verify the autofilled fields, and click the Save button.
Expected Result: The form data is saved successfully, and the modal closes, take user to the create invoice screen

Test Case Description: Click the Close button without entering or selecting any data.
Expected Result: The save button should be disabled

Test Case Description: Verify that the Buyer field is pre-populated with the buyer's name and cannot be modified.
Expected Result: The Buyer field should be pre-populated and non-editable.

Test Case Description: Select an address from the dropdown associated with the buyer.
Expected Result: The system should auto-fill the Pincode, District, and State fields based on the selected address.

Test Case Description: Check the “Additional Billing Address” checkbox and enter a new address within the state.
Expected Result: A form should appear to enter a new address. The Pincode, District, and State fields should be auto-filled based on the entered Pincode.

Test Case Description: Enter a custom pincode when the “Additional Billing Address” checkbox is checked.
Expected Result: The District and State should auto-fill based on the entered Pincode.

Test Case Description: Verify that the GST field is displayed and non-editable after selecting the buyer.
Expected Result: The GST field should be auto-filled with the buyer's GST number and should be non-editable.

Test Case Description: Click the "Save" button after entering/selecting all the necessary details (address, pincode, district, etc.).
Expected Result: The address should be saved and the user should be redirected to the "Create Invoice" screen.

Test Case Description: Click the "Close" button without saving any data.
Expected Result: The modal should close and the user should be redirected to the "Create Invoice" screen without saving any entered data.

Test Case Description: Enter an invalid pincode in the Additional Billing Address form.
Expected Result: The system should show an error message indicating the pincode is invalid or not recognized.

Test Case Description: Try to save the address without filling in the mandatory fields (e.g., Pincode, State).
Expected Result: The system should show an error indicating missing required fields.

Test Case Description: Select the address dropdown without any previously saved addresses for the selected buyer.
Expected Result: The system should display a message stating "No previous addresses available" or similar.

Test Case Description: Verify the supplier name in shipping from
Expected Result: Supplier name should be always "Farmart Services Private Limited"

Test Case Description: Verify the address and pin code field
Expected Result: Shipping from address may be different from billing from address, but state should be same as billing from state

Test Case Description: Verify shipping to address
Expected Result: Shipping to address should always be same as address provide while SO is created

Test Case Description: Verify user is able to edit shipping to address or not
Expected Result: Edit Shipping to address should always be disabled or not allow to edit it

Test Scenario: Verify invoice and e-way bill creation on Portal 1 when it is operational
Test Case Description: Create an invoice and generate an e-way bill while Portal 1 is up and running
Expected Result: Invoice and e-way bill should be successfully generated using Portal 1

Test Scenario: Verify automatic switch to Portal 2 when Portal 1 is down
Test Case Description: Shut down Portal 1 and attempt to create an invoice and generate an e-way bill
Expected Result: The system should automatically switch to Portal 2 and process the invoice and e-way bill successfully

Test Scenario: Verify system reverts back to Portal 1 when it becomes operational
Test Case Description: Restart Portal 1 after the system has switched to Portal 2, then create a new invoice and generate an e-way bill
Expected Result: The system should switch back to Portal 1 and process the invoice and e-way bill successfully

Test Scenario: Verify that an invoice cannot be generated if both Portal 1 and Portal 2 are down
Test Case Description: Shut down both portals and attempt to create an invoice and generate an e-way bill
Expected Result: The system should display an appropriate error message and prevent invoice generation

Test Scenario: Verify system logs and error messages when switching between portals
Test Case Description: Simulate Portal 1 going down and ensure logs and error messages are correctly recorded when switching to Portal 2 and back
Expected Result: The system logs should capture the downtime, switch-over, and restoration events accurately

Test Scenario: Verify partial invoice creation when Portal 1 goes down mid-process
Test Case Description: Start invoice creation on Portal 1, then simulate Portal 1 failure mid-process
Expected Result: The system should automatically switch to Portal 2 and resume invoice creation from the last successful step

Test Scenario: Verify invoice duplication prevention when switching between portals
Test Case Description: Create an invoice on Portal 1, then simulate Portal 1 failure and retry the same invoice creation on Portal 2
Expected Result: The system should prevent duplicate invoice creation and handle retry scenarios effectively

Test Scenario: Verify system behavior during high load while switching portals
Test Case Description: Simulate high traffic load and force Portal 1 failure, triggering a switch to Portal 2
Expected Result: The system should handle load efficiently and switch to Portal 2 without delays or failures

Test Scenario: Verify system notification when switching between portals
Test Case Description: Simulate Portal 1 going down and check if a notification is triggered for users/admins
Expected Result: The system should send a notification or alert to relevant users/admins about the portal switch

Test Scenario: Verify invoice creation when Portal 1 is down and comes back up during invoice processing
Test Case Description: Start invoice creation on Portal 2 while Portal 1 is down, then bring Portal 1 back online mid-process
Expected Result: The invoice should continue processing on Portal 2 without interruption, and subsequent invoices should default to Portal 1

Test Scenario: Verify timeout handling when switching between portals
Test Case Description: Intentionally slow down the portal switch-over process and attempt invoice creation
Expected Result: The system should gracefully handle delays, display a message if needed, and retry the switch without failures

Test Scenario: Verify system recovery if both portals go down and then come back online
Test Case Description: Shut down both portals, attempt invoice creation, then bring them back online
Expected Result: The system should recover without data loss and allow invoice creation when at least one portal is up

Test Scenario: Verify audit trail and logging of portal switch events
Test Case Description: Switch between portals multiple times and review system logs
Expected Result: The system logs should accurately capture every portal switch event with timestamps and reasons

Test Scenario: Verify user session persistence when switching portals
Test Case Description: Log in and begin invoice creation, then simulate Portal 1 failure mid-session
Expected Result: The user session should remain active, and the system should switch seamlessly to Portal 2 without requiring re-login

Test Scenario: Verify API response time when switching portals
Test Case Description: Measure API response time when switching from Portal 1 to Portal 2
Expected Result: The API response time should remain within acceptable limits (< X ms) and not degrade significantly

Test Scenario: Verify that Farmart Buyer (User for Inventory) does not appear in the Request Invoice Listing screen
Test Case Description: Create an SO for a Farmart Buyer and navigate to the Request Invoice Listing screen
Expected Result: The Farmart Buyer should not be listed in the Request Invoice screen

Test Scenario: Verify that non-Farmart buyers are displayed in the Request Invoice Listing screen
Test Case Description: Create an SO for a regular buyer and navigate to the Request Invoice Listing screen
Expected Result: The regular buyer should be listed in the Request Invoice screen

Test Scenario: Verify invoice creation with spaces in input fields
Test Case Description: Enter values with leading/trailing spaces in any invoice field and create the invoice
Expected Result: The invoice should be created, but spaces should be trimmed in the final invoice document

Test Scenario: Verify invoice creation with tab characters in input fields
Test Case Description: Enter values with tab spaces in fields and create the invoice
Expected Result: The invoice should be created, but tab spaces should be removed from the final invoice document

Test Scenario: Verify invoice creation with line breaks (Enter key) in input fields
Test Case Description: Enter multiple lines using the Enter key in fields like Address, Remarks, etc., and create the invoice
Expected Result: The invoice should be created, but line breaks should be removed in the final invoice document

Test Scenario: Verify invoice document output when special characters are entered
Test Case Description: Create an invoice with spaces, tabs, and line breaks in fields and generate the final invoice document
Expected Result: The final invoice document should display values correctly without unnecessary spaces, tabs, or line breaks

Test Scenario: Verify system validation message for excessive special characters
Test Case Description: Enter excessive spaces, tabs, or line breaks in required fields and try to submit
Expected Result: The system should either auto-trim or show a warning message before submission

Test Scenario: Verify database storage of invoice details when special characters are entered
Test Case Description: Enter an invoice with spaces, tabs, and line breaks, then check the stored database values
Expected Result: The database should store trimmed values without unnecessary spaces or line breaks

Test Scenario: Verify E-Way Bill creation when the server is up
Test Case Description: Generate an E-Way Bill when the system server and GST portal are operational
Expected Result: The system should successfully push data to GST and generate the E-Way Bill

Test Scenario: Verify error handling when the GST portal is down
Test Case Description: Attempt to generate an E-Way Bill when the GST portal is down
Expected Result: The system should display a proper error message indicating the GST portal is unavailable

Test Scenario: Verify retry mechanism when GST portal is temporarily down
Test Case Description: Simulate GST portal downtime and check if the system retries E-Way Bill creation after some time
Expected Result: The system should retry pushing data after a predefined interval until the portal is back up

Test Scenario: Verify error handling when API fails due to changes in API endpoints
Test Case Description: Simulate a failure in API due to endpoint changes and attempt to generate an E-Way Bill
Expected Result: The system should display an appropriate error message indicating API failure due to endpoint changes

Test Scenario: Verify system logs capture GST portal downtime and API failures
Test Case Description: Simulate both GST portal downtime and API endpoint changes, then check system logs
Expected Result: The logs should accurately record failures, reasons, and timestamps for debugging

Test Scenario: Verify system behavior when the E-Way Bill generation request is pending due to downtime
Test Case Description: Attempt to create an E-Way Bill when the GST portal is down, then check system behavior once it's back up
Expected Result: The system should either auto-push pending requests or allow manual retry once GST is available

Test Scenario: Verify error message format and clarity for users
Test Case Description: Trigger API failures and GST portal downtime and observe displayed error messages
Expected Result: Error messages should be user-friendly, clearly state the issue, and suggest possible actions
